seoTitle: Glastonbury vs Glen Allyn — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Glastonbury and Glen Allyn. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Glastonbury vs Glen Allyn: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Glastonbury and Glen Allyn.
heading: Glastonbury vs Glen Allyn — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Glastonbury, Queensland and Glen Allyn, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Glen Allyn has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Glastonbury (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Glastonbury or Glen Allyn safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Glastonbury has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Glen Allyn scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Glastonbury scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Glen Allyn scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Glastonbury or Glen Allyn?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Glen Allyn has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
